canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, DUDE is consistently presented as a benchmark for multi-page document understanding. It is explicitly used to measure the construct of 'Long-context understanding', with one paper further specifying its use in evaluating retrieval and question-answering (QA) performance. The dataset is described as containing financial reports and is noted for its 'diverse domains and complex document structures' ("Enhancing Large Language Model for Knowledge Graph Completion via Structure-Aware Alignment-Tuning"). The task format is identified as multi-page visual question answering. A commonly mentioned characteristic is its focus on documents up to 20 pages; one study explicitly states that DUDE does 'not assess documents exceeding 20 pages' ("BIPro: Zero-shotChinese Poem Generation via Block Inverse Prompting Constrained Generation Framework"). In evaluations, it is sometimes used alongside other document understanding datasets such as MP-DocVQA.
papers: BIPro: Zero-shotChinese Poem Generation via Block Inverse Prompting Constrained Generation Framework
Efficient Universal Goal Hijacking with Semantics-guided Prompt Organization
On the Relation Between Fine-Tuning, Topological Properties, and Task Performance in Sense-Enhanced Embeddings
AutoCT: Automating Interpretable Clinical Trial Prediction withLLMAgents
Enhancing Large Language Model for Knowledge Graph Completion via Structure-Aware Alignment-Tuning
The Pursuit of Empathy: Evaluating Small Language Models forPTSDDialogue Support

definitions.text: A benchmark for multi-page document understanding, for documents up to 20 pages.
definitions.paper: BIPro: Zero-shotChinese Poem Generation via Block Inverse Prompting Constrained Generation Framework

relationships.paper: MMLONGBENCH-DOC: Benchmarking Long-context Document Understanding with Visualizations
